name: performance-analysis
description: Comprehensive performance analysis, bottleneck detection, and optimization recommendations for Claude Flow swarms
capabilities: Detect performance bottlenecks across communication, processing, memory, and network layers
Generate comprehensive performance reports in JSON, HTML, or Markdown formats
Analyze agent utilization, task completion times, and coordination overhead
Provide AI-powered optimization recommendations with estimated improvement percentages
Apply automatic optimizations with --fix flag for common issues
Compare current performance with historical data to track improvements
author: Claude Flow Team
securitySummary: This skill contains only documentation and usage examples for the Claude Flow performance analysis CLI. All static findings are false positives. The flagged 'weak cryptographic algorithm' patterns are skill tags (optimization, analysis) in JSON metadata. The 'external commands' and 'child_process' patterns are markdown code blocks showing example CLI usage, not executable code. The 'path traversal' patterns are documentation links. No executable code, no data exfiltration, no malicious patterns. Pure documentation skill.
valueStatement: Claude Flow swarms can experience performance issues affecting speed and efficiency. This skill detects bottlenecks across communication, processing, memory, and network layers. It generates detailed performance reports and provides actionable optimization recommendations to improve swarm performance.
limitations: Requires Claude Flow to be installed and configured for full functionality
Does not modify source code directly; only provides recommendations
Cannot diagnose issues outside of Claude Flow swarm operations
Performance metrics depend on proper logging and monitoring setup

useCases.title: Monitor production swarms
useCases.description: Track performance metrics and detect issues in production Claude Flow swarm deployments before they impact users

useCases.title: Review team performance
useCases.description: Generate performance reports to evaluate team efficiency and identify optimization opportunities across projects

useCases.title: Optimize swarm efficiency
useCases.description: Fine-tune swarm topology and agent configurations based on bottleneck analysis and improvement suggestions

promptTemplates.title: Quick health check
promptTemplates.prompt: Run a quick bottleneck detection on the current swarm with default settings

promptTemplates.title: Detailed analysis
promptTemplates.prompt: Generate a comprehensive performance report for the last 24 hours including all metrics and recommendations

promptTemplates.title: Fix issues
promptTemplates.prompt: Detect bottlenecks with a 15 percent threshold and apply automatic fixes to resolve critical issues

promptTemplates.title: CI/CD monitoring
promptTemplates.prompt: Export bottleneck analysis to JSON format and fail if critical issues are detected above 10 percent threshold

bestPractices: Run bottleneck detection after major changes to identify performance regressions
Set appropriate thresholds: 10-15% for production, 25-30% for development environments
Review recommendations before applying --fix to understand the changes being made
antiPatterns: Running with default threshold without considering your specific performance requirements
Ignoring warning-level bottlenecks that can escalate to critical issues over time

faq.question: Which Claude tools are supported?
faq.answer: This skill works with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code. Full functionality requires Claude Flow installed locally.

faq.question: What performance metrics are analyzed?
faq.answer: Communication delays, task completion times, agent utilization, cache hit rates, memory access patterns, and network latency.

faq.question: Can this skill modify my code?
faq.answer: No. The skill only analyzes performance and provides recommendations. The --fix flag adjusts swarm configuration, not source code.

faq.question: Is my data safe?
faq.answer: Yes. All analysis runs locally using your Claude Flow metrics. No data is sent to external servers.

faq.question: Why are no bottlenecks detected?
faq.answer: Check that Claude Flow logging is enabled and metrics are being recorded. Ensure sufficient historical data exists for analysis.

faq.question: How does this compare to other monitoring tools?
faq.answer: This skill is specialized for Claude Flow swarms with AI-powered recommendations. Use general tools for system-level monitoring.
